skill.name: improving-components
skill.description: Applies structured improvements to Claude Code components using quality analysis results. It refines descriptions, permissions, triggers, examples, and safety guidance while preserving component intent.
skill.summary: Improve Claude Code components with safer permissions, clearer triggers, and better documentation.
skill.author: C0ntr0lledCha0s

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance alerts, but review shows they are false positives in markdown examples and prose. No prompt injection, network access, shell execution permission, or malicious intent was found. The remaining risk is low because the skill intentionally has Edit access for component improvement work.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Modification Permission
low_findings.description: The skill declares Read and Edit permissions, so it can modify existing component files. This matches the documented purpose of applying improvements and is not evidence of malicious behavior, but users should review changes before accepting them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools line directly grants Edit access. The surrounding documentation consistently limits this access to improving component files, so the concern is capability risk rather than malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Ruby or Shell Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: The external command alerts point to markdown code fences and illustrative examples, not runnable Ruby backticks or shell execution. The skill does not request Bash permission and does not contain operational command execution logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows these alerts are inside documentation blocks or markdown formatting. The frontmatter grants only Read and Edit, which removes the direct shell execution path reported by the scanner.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts are triggered by ordinary prose, YAML descriptions, markdown filenames, and template text. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic implementation in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed the reported lines and found documentation text rather than crypto APIs or algorithm recommendations. The scanner appears to have matched short substrings in words such as description or markdown-related text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Terminology
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance alerts are caused by phrases about avoiding false positives, removing unnecessary Bash access, and validating YAML frontmatter. No scanning, probing, host discovery, or network instruction is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are quality and validation guidance, not reconnaissance procedures. The skill has no WebFetch, WebSearch, Bash, or network tool permission.

content.value_statement: Claude Code components can drift into vague descriptions, excessive permissions, and weak examples. This skill turns quality analysis into focused edits that improve clarity, safety, and usability.
actual_capabilities: Refines skill, agent, command, and hook descriptions with clearer activation triggers.
Reviews allowed tools and recommends smaller permission sets when functionality allows it.
Adds or improves usage examples, capability lists, and documentation structure.
Applies improvements incrementally while preserving the component purpose and interface.
Documents before and after changes with quality impact and remaining recommendations.
limitations: Requires quality analysis or a clear improvement target before making edits.
Can edit existing files but does not create a full component system from scratch.
Does not run external validation scripts unless another tool or user workflow provides them.
Cannot guarantee marketplace acceptance without separate policy and security review.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Skill for Marketplace Review
use_cases.description: Improve descriptions, permissions, examples, and safety notes before submitting a Claude Code skill.

use_cases.title: Reduce Tool Permissions
use_cases.description: Review a component that requests broad tools and narrow the list to what the workflow needs.

use_cases.title: Standardize Component Documentation
use_cases.description: Apply consistent structure, triggers, capabilities, and examples across agents, commands, hooks, and skills.

prompt_templates.title: Improve One Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single file
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve this Claude Code component for clarity, safety, and usability. Preserve its purpose and explain each change.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Quality Findings
prompt_templates.prompt: Use these quality analysis findings to update the component. Apply critical fixes first, then improve descriptions and examples.

prompt_templates.title: Tighten Permissions
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the allowed tools for this component. Remove permissions that are not required and document why the remaining tools are needed.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Marketplace Submission
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare this component for marketplace submission. Improve triggers, documentation, examples, security posture, and the final change summary.

output_examples.input: A skill has a vague description and broad tool permissions.
output: A specific description with activation triggers.
A smaller tool list with a short permission rationale.
A summary of security and usability improvements.

output_examples.input: An agent lacks examples and has unclear scope.
output: Practical usage examples for target scenarios.
Boundaries that explain when not to use the agent.

output_examples.input: A component needs marketplace readiness review.
output: Prioritized edits for critical, important, and optional improvements.

best_practices: Start from existing quality findings and keep each edit tied to a specific issue.
Review permission changes carefully so required functionality is not removed.
Check the final component manually for preserved intent, clear triggers, and valid frontmatter.
anti_patterns: Do not rewrite the component purpose while trying to improve style.
Do not keep broad tools only because they might be useful later.
Do not add examples that imply capabilities the component does not support.

faq.question: What does this skill improve?
faq.answer: It improves Claude Code component descriptions, permissions, triggers, documentation, examples, and safety guidance.

faq.question: Does it change how a component works?
faq.answer: It is designed to preserve the component purpose and interface while improving quality around it.

faq.question: Can it remove tool permissions?
faq.answer: Yes. It can recommend or apply smaller tool lists when the removed tools are not needed.

faq.question: Does it run scripts automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill documentation mentions validation scripts as examples, but the skill only declares Read and Edit tools.

faq.question: Is it useful without a quality report?
faq.answer: Yes, but it works best when the user provides findings, goals, or examples of weak component behavior.

faq.question: Who should review the output?
faq.answer: The component owner should review all edits, especially permission changes and marketplace-facing descriptions.
